title: Tanarius hosei
intro: Tanarius hosei, also known as Tanacetum parthenium or Feverfew, is a flowering plant in the sunflower family that has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Tanarius hosei include its ability to alleviate headaches and migraines due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Tanarius hosei include sesquiterpene lactones, such as parthenolide, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tanarius hosei include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support the body's natural response to fever and inflammation.
taxonomy: Tanarius hosei is a species of ground spider within the family Gnaphosidae. The classification of this spider places it within the phylum Arthropoda, specifically the class Arachnida. Within the class Arachnida, Tanarius hosei is further classified as an order Araneomorphae and a family Gnaphosidae. The family Gnaphosidae is then divided into the subfamily Gnaphosinae. Tanarius hosei is a member of the genus Tanarius, which is also a part of the family Gnaphosidae.
